questions.question: How long does your CDISC dataset preparation take from raw data to submission-ready — days, weeks, or months?
questions.intent: CDISC prep time is a major pain. SAS Life Science Analytics automates SDTM/ADaM generation.
questions.followUp: How many person-hours go into a single submission dataset?

questions.question: What are the biggest bottlenecks in your regulatory submission process — data preparation, statistical analysis, or review cycles?
questions.intent: Identifies where SAS can accelerate the pipeline. Often it's all three.
questions.followUp: How many submissions do you file annually and what's the average cycle time?

questions.question: Are you using real-world evidence to supplement clinical trial data — EHR mining, claims data, or registry linkage?
questions.intent: RWE is a growing requirement from regulators. SAS handles unstructured health data at scale.
questions.followUp: What data sources do you have access to for RWE studies?

questions.question: How do you handle pharmacovigilance signal detection — automated statistical monitoring or manual periodic review?
questions.intent: Drug safety monitoring at scale requires analytics. SAS automates signal detection and disproportionality analysis.
questions.followUp: How many adverse event reports do you process and what's your detection latency?

questions.question: How are you optimizing clinical trial design — adaptive trials, Bayesian methods, or traditional fixed protocols?
questions.intent: Adaptive trial design reduces time and cost. SAS has best-in-class statistical procedures for this.
questions.followUp: What's your average trial duration and patient enrollment rate?

questions.question: How do you ensure reproducibility in your statistical analyses — is there a validated computing environment with audit trails?
questions.intent: Reproducibility is a regulatory requirement. SAS provides validated, auditable statistical computing.
questions.followUp: Have regulators ever questioned the reproducibility of your submitted analyses?

questions.question: Are you still running on SAS 9.4 for clinical workloads — have you evaluated the migration path to Viya?
questions.intent: Many life sciences companies are on SAS 9.4. Migration to Viya is an upsell opportunity.
questions.followUp: What's blocking the migration — validation requirements, existing macros, or resource constraints?
